world.title: Not going anywhere: Iran rejects Trump's plan to transfer uranium to US
world.summary: Iran has rejected U.S. President Donald Trump's assertion that it plans to transfer enriched uranium to the United States. This denial comes amid ongoing negotiations between Washington and Tehran, which continue to face significant disagreements over nuclear material. Despite indications of potential progress, the fate of Iran's enriched uranium remains a contentious issue in the discussions.

world.title: Oil companies, exporters, importers cheer Hormuz Strait opening
world.summary: Indian oil companies, along with fertiliser importers and exporters, are celebrating the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz for global commercial vessels. This development allows for 41 ships, both Indian and foreign-flagged, to transport cargo, including crude oil, to India. The reopening is expected to facilitate trade and enhance supply chains for essential commodities in the region.

world.title: Next round of US-Iran peace talks could take place in Pakistan on Monday: Report
world.summary: A new round of US-Iran peace talks is anticipated in Pakistan on Monday, according to Iranian sources. While President Trump has expressed optimism about nearing a deal, significant issues remain unresolved. Delegations from both nations are expected to participate in the discussions, aiming to address these sticking points and advance diplomatic relations between the two countries.

business.title: Why global tech firms have cut over 73,000 jobs in 2026 so far?
business.summary: In 2026, global tech firms have cut over 73,000 jobs, driven primarily by restructuring related to artificial intelligence. Companies are reallocating resources towards automation, infrastructure, and efficiency, while downsizing traditional business units. This trend reflects an ongoing reset in the technology sector following the pandemic, as firms adapt to changing market demands and operational strategies.

business.title: AI Chief Says Software Engineer Jobs No More, But…
business.summary: An AI chief has stated that software engineer jobs may become obsolete due to advancements in artificial intelligence. This development raises concerns about the future of the human workforce, as AI technology continues to evolve and impact employment opportunities globally. The transition towards AI engineering is prompting discussions about the implications for workers in various sectors.

business.title: reactors in space orbit by 2028, on moon in 2030
business.summary: The Trump administration announced plans to deploy nuclear reactors in space orbit by 2028 and on the Moon by 2030. This memorandum was issued three days after the successful splashdown of Artemis-2. The initiative aims to enhance energy capabilities for future space missions and support sustained human presence in space, reflecting ongoing advancements in space exploration technology.

technology.title: I swapped my phone for a cycle computer after a crash - but a tiny, underrated device was the real safety upgrade
technology.summary: After experiencing multiple minor bike crashes, the author replaced their smartphone with a cycle computer, which improved focus while cycling. The real safety upgrade, however, came from using a small, underrated device that enhanced awareness of surrounding vehicles, particularly trucks and cars. This change has contributed to a safer cycling experience by reducing distractions and increasing vigilance on the road.

technology.title: How To Unlock Free Fortnite Jess Skin This Season
technology.summary: A new free Fortnite skin, the Jess skin, is available this season. Players can unlock it by participating in the Save the World mode before June. This addition coincides with the Save the World mode becoming free to play. The latest Fortnite update includes various new content, but the Jess skin is a notable highlight.

technology.title: Apple may launch iPhone 18 Pro models in new darker colours: What to expect
technology.summary: Apple is anticipated to launch the iPhone 18 Pro models with a new darker color palette. Supply chain leaks suggest that these refreshed colors will be part of the upcoming lineup. The new models are expected to be unveiled later this year, following the previous iPhone 17 Pro release. Details about specific colors remain unconfirmed at this time.

sports.title: ‘This is what my mother wanted’: Behind Lokesh Sathyanathan’s NCAA-winning 8.21m leap
sports.summary: Lokesh Sathyanathan achieved an 8.21m leap in Fayetteville, breaking his national record and becoming the fourth Indian to win an NCAA Division I title. This accomplishment places him third on India's all-time list. His success follows a challenging period marked by injuries and personal loss, reflecting his determination and dedication to the sport, fulfilling a promise to his mother.

sports.title: Cole Palmer opens up on Man United transfer links as Chelsea star reveals talks with owners
sports.summary: Chelsea's Cole Palmer has stated he has "no plans" to leave Stamford Bridge amid transfer rumors linking him to Manchester United. Earlier reports suggested he was open to returning to Manchester due to feeling unsettled in London. However, Palmer has clarified his commitment to Chelsea, emphasizing his intention to remain with the club for the foreseeable future.

sports.title: Wolvaardt, Dercksen seal SA chase after India stumble with bat
sports.summary: South Africa Women defeated India Women by six wickets in Durban, successfully chasing a target of 158 runs. India struggled with a sloppy batting performance, finishing at 157 for 7, with Harmanpreet scoring 47 not out. South Africa's Wolvaardt contributed 51 runs, while Dercksen remained unbeaten on 44. Khaka excelled with the ball, taking 3 wickets for 16 runs.

health.title: How summers can add stress to your liver
health.summary: Hot summer weather can stress the liver due to increased heat, dehydration, and dietary changes. As temperatures rise, individuals often focus on staying cool but neglect liver health. Proper hydration and a balanced diet are essential to support liver function during summer months. Awareness of these factors can help mitigate potential liver-related issues associated with the season.

health.title: First HMPV case detected in Maharashtra this year recovers
health.summary: Maharashtra has reported its first Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) case of the year, involving a seven-month-old boy from Washim who tested positive on March 30, 2026. He was admitted to AIIMS Nagpur for treatment and has since recovered, being discharged recently. Health officials confirmed this as the first confirmed HMPV case in the state for 2026.

health.title: Quick weight-loss fixes not enough, say docs
health.summary: Doctors caution that quick weight-loss methods, such as intermittent fasting and keto diets, are insufficient for effective weight management. They emphasize the importance of a balanced diet, strength training, and regular health checks for diabetes, blood pressure, and erectile dysfunction. Experts highlight that sustainable weight control requires a comprehensive approach rather than relying solely on rapid fixes.

science.title: Archaeologists Uncover Hidden 9,000-Year-Old Lost World Beneath the Great Lakes
science.summary: Archaeologists have discovered a 9,000-year-old lost world beneath Lake Huron, revealing insights into an ancient civilization. This finding, detailed in a study published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, uncovers a previously hidden chapter of North America's prehistory. The submerged landscape offers valuable information about early human life and environmental conditions in the region.

science.title: NASA’s Parker Probe captures the exact moment solar storms are born
science.summary: NASA’s Parker Solar Probe has achieved a significant milestone by capturing the first direct in-situ measurements of magnetic reconnection, which is the process that initiates solar storms. This groundbreaking observation allows scientists to understand the precise moment solar storms are born, enhancing knowledge of solar activity and its effects on space weather. The probe's findings could improve forecasting efforts.

science.title: Is supervolcano Yellowstone going to erupt anytime soon? New study analyses what's next
science.summary: New research indicates that Yellowstone's magma may be situated in shallow "magma mush" zones, rather than a single deep chamber. This finding could change eruption modeling, but scientists stress there are currently no signs of an imminent eruption. The study aims to enhance understanding of the supervolcano's behavior, which has long been a source of public interest and concern.

entertainment.title: Horoscope Today: April 18, 2026
entertainment.summary: On April 18, 2026, horoscopes suggest that Geminis possess the knowledge and solutions they need but struggle with self-doubt. They may question their voice, heart, and purpose. The guidance encourages them to trust themselves more, indicating that clarity and confidence are essential for moving forward. The stars emphasize the importance of overcoming internal uncertainties to achieve personal growth.

entertainment.title: Pisces Horoscope Today: April 18, 2026
entertainment.summary: Pisces individuals are encouraged to embrace a new idea that has recently come to them. Before pursuing multiple directions, it is advised to take a moment for self-reflection. Consider which path aligns best with personal values and aspirations. This approach may help in effectively bringing the idea to fruition while ensuring it resonates with one's true self.

entertainment.title: Libra Horoscope Today: April 18, 2026
entertainment.summary: Libra individuals are encouraged to engage in physical activity today, such as sprinting or brisk walking, to invigorate their bodies. Exercise is emphasized as a way to feel more alive and energized. Additionally, it is advised to opt for lighter meals to complement the focus on health and wellness. Overall, today is about movement and mindful eating.
